campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a public hearing on July 7, 2026, to consider revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for at least two smoke shops in the city. The council also discussed adopting a new interim ordinance related to Senate legislation and approving consultant service agreements for a public works grant submission.
keyTopics: Revoking tobacco retailer permits for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop
New interim ordinance superseding previous tobacco-related regulations
Public Works grant submission authorization
Consultant service agreements approval

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved preliminary engineering reports and set up a public hearing for a new ordinance, with a final vote scheduled for May 20th. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed different tax options and revenue estimates to help guide that decision.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report preliminary approval for the fiscal year
Public hearing scheduled for new ordinance
Exploring charter city status for Saratoga
Tax options and revenue estimates under review

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council held their April 21, 2026 meeting to approve financial reports, authorize construction and service contracts, and hear public appeals on unspecified matters following a court order.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 monthly financial and investment report
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Heard public appeals following court order (Johnathan Heartnett and others)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several city matters including designating a historic property at 647 South Sixth Street as a landmark, considering a change to how the city fills vacant mayor or council seats using ranked choice voting, and approving contracts for airport advertising and long-term power supply.
keyTopics: Historic landmark designation and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Proposed charter amendment to allow ranked choice voting for filling mayor and council vacancies
Large format advertising sign contract at San José Mineta International Airport
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, to discuss hiring a new City Attorney and address legal matters. The council also reviewed a residential development proposal for 333 Franklin Street.
keyTopics: City Attorney position - hiring and appointment
Residential development project at 333 Franklin Street
Ongoing lawsuit: San Francisco Baykeeper v. City of Mountain View and City of Sunnyvale (federal court case)
Anticipated litigation - details being reviewed with legal counsel

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The closed session allowed council members to discuss confidential matters privately before the public meeting.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held before regular council session
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the city's Health and Safety Element, which is part of the long-range General Plan guiding development through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across all divisions and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad team for their achievement.
keyTopics: Updating Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department across all divisions
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — July 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on July 16, 2026 included a disclosure about legal deadlines for residents who want to challenge city decisions. If the city makes a quasi-adjudicative decision (like a permit denial or land-use ruling), residents have 90 days from the final decision to file a lawsuit, unless state law sets a shorter deadline.
keyTopics: 90-day window to sue the city over permit and land-use decisions
Legal challenges governed by California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
Deadline starts when a city decision becomes final

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to update their long-term housing and community development plans for 2025-2030, review requests from individual council members for new work items, and consider changes to how the city fills council vacancies. The council also approved meeting minutes and scheduled upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (housing and federal funding priorities)
Council members' new workplan requests and priorities
Proposed changes to council vacancy appointment process
Upcoming city council meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The council honored Bill Gutoff's retirement and held a public hearing on proposed rate increases for water, sewer, and trash services for the upcoming fiscal year. The Finance Committee is recommending approval of seven resolutions that would adjust utility rates and manage reserve funds.
keyTopics: Water rate increases for Fiscal Year 2027
Wastewater collection rate adjustments
Trash/refuse utility rate changes
Utility reserve management and fund transfers
Retirement recognition for Bill Gutoff
